factors.summary: Geopolitical risks such as wars, strikes, civil unrest, acts of terrorism, and other conflicts pose a threat to international trade.

evidence.text: changes and uncertainties in governmental policies and inter-governmental disputes, which could result in increased tariff rates, quota restrictions, trade barriers and other types of restrictions;
evidence.subsection: Any reduction in international commerce or disruption in global trade may adversely impact our business and operating results.

factors.summary: Economic conditions, including changes in labor costs, inflation, and consumer attitudes, may negatively impact the company's operating results.

evidence.text: changes in labor and other costs including the potential impacts of inflation; increased global concerns regarding working conditions and environmental sustainability;
evidence.subsection: Any reduction in international commerce or disruption in global trade may adversely impact our business and operating results.

factors.summary: The competitive environment is intensifying with many competitors actively pursuing new technologies and contractual terms to gain advantages.

evidence.text: The global logistics services industry is intensely competitive and is expected to remain so for the foreseeable future. ... Many larger customers utilize the services of multiple logistics providers. Customers regularly solicit bids from competitors in order to improve service and to secure favorable pricing and contractual terms such as longer payment terms, fixed-price arrangements, higher or unlimited liability limits and performance penalties.
evidence.subsection: Our industry is highly competitive, and failure to compete or respond to customer requirements could damage our business and results of operations.

factors.summary: The company faces challenges in retaining employees due to the shift to remote work and the desire of some employees to continue working remotely post-pandemic.

evidence.text: As the pandemic restrictions ease, we are requiring employees to return to the office. As a result, for those individuals that prefer working remotely, we may experience a higher degree of turnover and lower employee satisfaction in the near future.
evidence.subsection: We are dependent on our personnel and any inability to hire, develop or retain our employees may have a negative impact on our operations.

factors.summary: The company is dependent on third-party service providers for transportation capacity, which may not always be sufficient to meet customer needs, impacting operations.

evidence.text: Our ability to deliver our services depends on service providers’ having sufficient capacity available to purchase. When market demand significantly exceeds available capacity in a given market, which has been increasingly the case for various services and markets since the beginning of the pandemic in 2020, we may not always be able to find acceptable transportation solutions to meet our customers’ needs or the routing and delivery of freight may be subject to delays that are outside of our control.
evidence.subsection: We are dependent on our personnel and any inability to hire, develop or retain our employees may have a negative impact on our operations.
